So LLMs like freshness.
So usually if a piece of content is older than six months, then it kind of starts dropping off.
But typically, you know, once.
Once it's in Google and once it's ranking well, it starts to show up.
An interesting thing about LLMs is that an article could be ranking on page 7 or 8 for the target keyword, but it might get picked up by LLMs and be cited depending on the prompt.

So they're not really looking at where the content is ranked by Google, but they're actually looking at the content on the page itself and whether the information is presented clearly so that they can like neatly kind of repackage it and serve it back up to the user.
